about.title: About BiblioFuse
about.intro_1: BiblioFuse is a suite of tools and readers designed to give e-book lovers complete control over their library. Unlike walled gardens that lock you into a specific ecosystem, BiblioFuse empowers you to manage, convert, and read your books on any device.
about.intro_2: Our web tool uses cutting-edge WebAssembly (WASM) technology to process files entirely on your device. This means private, fast, and secure file management without ever uploading your personal library to a server.
about.work_text: We are a small, passionate team dedicated to building software that respects user privacy and ownership. We are currently open to freelance opportunities and collaborations.
about.connect_text: Interested in contributing or hiring? Reach out to us.

home.title: Manage & Read E-Books Anywhere
home.desc: BiblioFuse: Offline ebook reader & library manager. Tag, rate, track reading progress. Private personal library with iCloud sync. No ads, no tracking. CBZ, EPUB, ZIP. iOS, Android, Windows.

reader.title: Next-Gen E-Book Reader
reader.desc: A fast, distraction-free e-book reader for iOS and Android. Features continuous webtoon mode, smart tagging, and Peek Zoom for comics.

about.title: About Us
about.desc: Learn about the team behind BiblioFuse. We are dedicated to building private, user-first software for e-book lovers.

noAds.title: No Ads

noTracking.desc: Your privacy is protected. We don't collect or sell your data

double-page-spreads-comics.title: Reading Double-Page Spreads in Comics: How BiblioFuse Handles Wide Images
double-page-spreads-comics.excerpt: BiblioFuse reads CBZ, CBR, EPUB, PDF, ZIP, RAR, and TXT files on iPhone and iPad running iOS 17 or later. When you reach a double-page spread, BiblioFuse automatically detects paired pages and can display them side-by-side, preserving the intended artwork. You can switch between single-page, side-by-side, and full-zoom modes at any time.
double-page-spreads-comics.seoDescription: Learn how BiblioFuse handles double-page spreads in comics on iPhone and iPad. Automatic spread detection, landscape mode, and pinch-to-zoom for the best reading experience.

sideload-comics-iphone-without-itunes.title: How to Sideload Comics to iPhone Without iTunes or iCloud
sideload-comics-iphone-without-itunes.excerpt: BiblioFuse reads CBZ, CBR, EPUB, PDF, ZIP, RAR, and TXT files on iPhone and iPad running iOS 17 or later, and includes a built-in Wi-Fi Transfer feature so you can copy comics directly from any Mac or PC browser — no iTunes, no cable, and no iCloud account needed.
sideload-comics-iphone-without-itunes.seoDescription: Learn how to sideload comics to iPhone without iTunes using BiblioFuse Wi-Fi Transfer. No cable required — transfer CBZ, EPUB, and PDF files wirelessly from any browser.

ebook-formats-long-term-library.title: Best File Formats for a Long-Term Digital Library: EPUB, CBZ, and PDF Compared
ebook-formats-long-term-library.excerpt: BiblioFuse reads EPUB, CBZ, CBR, PDF, ZIP, RAR, and TXT files on iPhone and iPad running iOS 17 or later, as well as on Mac, making it a single reader for any long-term format you choose. For long-term preservation, EPUB is best for text books, CBZ is best for comics and manga, and PDF is a reliable fallback.
ebook-formats-long-term-library.seoDescription: Compare EPUB, CBZ, and PDF for long-term digital library preservation. Learn which open file formats keep your ebook and comic collection readable for decades.

backup-comic-library-mac.title: How to Back Up Your Comic Library on Mac: External Drive, NAS, and Cloud Options
backup-comic-library-mac.excerpt: BiblioFuse stores your comics and ebooks — EPUB, CBZ, CBR, PDF, ZIP, RAR, and TXT — on iPhone, iPad, and Mac, and streams your Mac library directly to iPhone over Wi-Fi without copying files. To back up your Mac comic library, you can use Time Machine with an external drive or NAS, copy files to cloud storage like iCloud Drive or Dropbox, or use any backup tool that mirrors a standard folder. BiblioFuse reading progress syncs automatically via iCloud so your place in every book is always preserved.
backup-comic-library-mac.seoDescription: Learn how to back up your Mac comic library using Time Machine, external drives, NAS, and cloud storage. Protect your EPUB, CBZ, and PDF collection from data loss.

extract-images-from-cbz.title: How to Extract Images from CBZ Files Online (Free, No Software)
extract-images-from-cbz.excerpt: BiblioFuse Web lets you extract individual images from CBZ files directly in your browser — no upload, no software, no account. Processing runs entirely in WebAssembly so your comics stay private.
extract-images-from-cbz.seoDescription: Extract images from CBZ files online for free using BiblioFuse Web. 100% browser-based with WebAssembly — no file upload, no server, no account required.

comic-bookmarks-iphone.title: Using Bookmarks in BiblioFuse: Never Lose Your Place in a Comic
comic-bookmarks-iphone.excerpt: BiblioFuse lets you drop bookmarks on any page in a comic or ebook on iPhone. Bookmarks sync across iPhone, iPad, and Mac via iCloud — your saved spots follow you across every device.
comic-bookmarks-iphone.seoDescription: Learn how to use bookmarks in BiblioFuse on iPhone to save your reading position in any comic or ebook. Bookmarks sync via iCloud across all your Apple devices.

night-reading-comics-iphone.title: Reading Comics at Night on iPhone: Dark Mode and Eye Comfort Tips
night-reading-comics-iphone.excerpt: BiblioFuse for iOS has dark mode, an in-app brightness slider below the iOS minimum, and a sepia filter — the right combination for nighttime comic and manga reading without eye strain.
night-reading-comics-iphone.seoDescription: BiblioFuse for iOS offers true dark mode, sub-iOS-minimum brightness, and a sepia filter for comfortable nighttime comic reading on iPhone.

cbz-to-pdf-online.excerpt: The BiblioFuse web app converts CBZ to PDF entirely in your browser using WebAssembly — no server, no account, no files uploaded anywhere. Instant conversion with no quality loss.
cbz-to-pdf-online.seoDescription: Convert CBZ to PDF free in your browser. BiblioFuse uses WebAssembly — no uploads, no account, no file size limits. Works on any device.

tailscale-remote-library-access.excerpt: Use BiblioFuse's home library streaming with Tailscale to read your entire Mac comic collection from anywhere — no cloud upload, no port forwarding, just an encrypted tunnel.
tailscale-remote-library-access.seoDescription: Stream your Mac comic library to iPhone from anywhere using BiblioFuse and Tailscale. No cloud upload, no static IP needed — just a secure VPN tunnel.

webp-vs-png-for-comics.title: WebP vs PNG for Comics: Best Quality-to-Size Ratio
webp-vs-png-for-comics.excerpt: WebP can cut your comic archive size by 70–80% vs PNG with no visible quality loss. A plain-English breakdown of when to use each format and how BiblioFuse handles both.
webp-vs-png-for-comics.seoDescription: Compare WebP vs PNG for comic and manga archives. Which format gives the best quality-to-size ratio? BiblioFuse reads both natively.

This guide covers organizing, compressing, transferring, and reading your entire digital comic library on iPhone, iPad, and Mac.

best-duplicate-file-finder-mac.title: Best Duplicate File Finder for Mac (2026)
best-duplicate-file-finder-mac.excerpt: Archive Duplicate Scanner is the best duplicate file finder for Mac in 2026 for mixed libraries of comics (CBZ, CBR), photos (HEIC, JPEG, RAW), and archives. Perceptual hashing finds visually identical files even after renaming or re-exporting, and Trash-only deletion keeps your collection safe.
best-duplicate-file-finder-mac.seoDescription: Archive Duplicate Scanner is the best duplicate file finder for Mac in 2026 for mixed libraries of comics (CBZ, CBR), photos (HEIC, JPEG, RAW).

find-duplicate-photos-mac.title: Find Duplicate Photos on Mac — Including HEIC, WebP, and RAW
find-duplicate-photos-mac.excerpt: Archive Duplicate Scanner finds duplicate photos on Mac using perceptual hashing, matching visually identical images across HEIC, JPEG, WebP, PNG, TIFF, and RAW formats even if they have different filenames or compression settings. Move duplicates safely to the Trash with auto-select strategies that keep the highest quality file.
find-duplicate-photos-mac.seoDescription: Archive Duplicate Scanner finds duplicate photos on Mac using perceptual hashing, matching visually identical images across HEIC, JPEG, WebP, PNG, TIFF.

perceptual-hashing-duplicate-detection.title: How Perceptual Hashing Finds Duplicate Comics Even After Renaming
perceptual-hashing-duplicate-detection.excerpt: Archive Duplicate Scanner uses perceptual hashing to find duplicate CBZ and CBR archives by comparing visual page content — not file checksums — so it catches duplicates that have been renamed, re-compressed, or downloaded from different sources. Understand how the algorithm works and why it outperforms standard checksum-based duplicate finders for manga and comic libraries.
perceptual-hashing-duplicate-detection.seoDescription: Archive Duplicate Scanner uses perceptual hashing to find duplicate CBZ and CBR archives by comparing visual page content — not file checksums — so it.

clean-up-duplicate-manga-library.title: How to Clean Up a Duplicate Manga Collection the Smart Way
clean-up-duplicate-manga-library.excerpt: Archive Duplicate Scanner finds duplicate manga volumes across your Mac using perceptual hashing, matching CBZ and CBR files even after renaming or recompression. Review matched pairs side by side, keep the best version, and safely move duplicates to Trash — no permanent deletion until you are ready.
clean-up-duplicate-manga-library.seoDescription: Archive Duplicate Scanner finds duplicate manga volumes across your Mac using perceptual hashing, matching CBZ and CBR files even after renaming.

find-duplicate-comics-cbz-mac.title: How to Find and Remove Duplicate CBZ Comic Files on Mac
find-duplicate-comics-cbz-mac.excerpt: Archive Duplicate Scanner finds duplicate CBZ and CBR comic files on Mac using perceptual hashing — matching files even after renaming, recompressing, or repackaging. find-duplicate-comics-cbz-mac.seoDescription: Archive Duplicate Scanner finds duplicate CBZ and CBR comic files on Mac using perceptual hashing — matching files even after renaming, recompressing.

home.seoTitle: BiblioFuse - E-book Reader, Library Manager & Compression Tools
home.seoDesc: BiblioFuse is a private e-book ecosystem for reading, organizing, streaming, compressing and merging your personal library on iPhone, iPad, Mac, Android and the web.

hero.version: Version 2.0.1 - native on iPhone, iPad & Mac
hero.desc: BiblioFuse is a native reader and library manager for personal ebook, comic, and document collections across iPhone, iPad, and Mac.
hero.warningBody: BiblioFuse is exclusively distributed via Apple App Store, Google Play Store and Microsoft Store. Never download from unofficial websites.

items.q: Does one purchase cover all platforms?
items.a: No. iOS + Mac is a single universal purchase — one payment covers iPhone, iPad, and Mac. The Android Reader, PC Reader, and PC tools are each sold separately.

items.q: How do I add books and folders to BiblioFuse on iPhone?
items.a: BiblioFuse stores files in its own iCloud-synced folder. You can add books via the Files app (copy files into the BiblioFuse folder), via AirDrop, or via the built-in Wi-Fi Transfer — open a library session from Mac or PC and drag files in from any browser.

items.q: How does streaming from Mac or PC to iPhone work?
items.a: Your Mac or PC runs a local server and writes the connection address to iCloud Drive. Your iPhone picks it up automatically. Both devices must be in the same Tailscale state: either both on Tailscale (works at home and remotely), or both off Tailscale (same Wi-Fi only, not fully tested). If only one device has Tailscale on, the connection will fail. Keeping both devices always on Tailscale gives the most consistent experience.

items.q: What do I need to set up streaming?
items.a: Both your Mac or PC and your iPhone must have the following configured:

bullets.text: Same iCloud account on both devices
sub: On iOS and Mac: go to Settings → [your name] → iCloud and make sure BiblioFuse is enabled
On PC: iCloud is not built-in — download iCloud for Windows from the Microsoft Store

bullets.text: Tailscale state must match on both devices — both ON or both OFF
sub: Both ON — works at home and remotely over 5G
Both OFF — same home Wi-Fi only (not fully tested)
Mixed (one ON, one OFF) — connection will fail
Tailscale exit node does not need to be enabled
iCloud and Tailscale accounts can use different email addresses

bullets.text: Allow network access when the app launches for the first time

items.q: Why does streaming require Tailscale? Can't it just use home Wi-Fi?
items.a: Tailscale is required because of how iOS handles local network connections. Here is what happened:

bullets.text: Early versions worked on home Wi-Fi without Tailscale
sub: The app used Bonjour (Apple's local discovery) over HTTP — this worked fine
Once remote 5G streaming was added, iOS App Transport Security began blocking HTTP to raw IP addresses, which broke the LAN-only path

bullets.text: Tailscale works on home Wi-Fi just as well as it does remotely
sub: When both devices are on the same network, Tailscale routes traffic directly — there is no speed penalty compared to plain Wi-Fi
Keeping Tailscale on at home and away gives you one consistent setup with no mode-switching

bullets.text: A Tailscale-free LAN mode is not in the current roadmap
sub: Even when you turn off Tailscale, the app may try the previously stored Tailscale address, causing a handshake failure
Building a reliable automatic LAN-only fallback is complex and not planned for now
The simplest setup: keep Tailscale on both devices always. It handles home Wi-Fi and 5G remote with zero extra configuration

items.q: Why can't Android receive books from Mac or PC?
items.a: The Android app uses a different codebase without iCloud integration, which is how the streaming handshake works. The best workflow for Android is to sync files to your phone using Syncthing or a similar tool, then add that folder as a library source in the app.

items.q: Where are reading progress, bookmarks, ratings, and tags saved?
items.a: It depends on where the book file lives:

bullets.text: Local storage — saved on that device only, no cross-device sync
iCloud storage — saved to iCloud, syncs across all iOS, Mac, and PC devices automatically
SMB / network share (Mac or PC streaming) — saved on the host device
sub: iOS reading a Mac stream: progress saves on the Mac
iOS reading a PC stream: progress saves on the PC
PC ↔ Mac: stored independently — they do not share data
If you use both Mac and PC as streaming hosts, your reading data will be split. Stick to one host for consistency.

items.q: What is the difference between the PC Reader and the PC EPUB / CBZ tools?
items.a: The PC Reader is a full reading app — it opens books, manages your library, compresses files, and streams to iPhone. The PC EPUB and CBZ tools are lighter, cheaper apps focused on compression and merging only, with no reader.

items.q: When will the PC Reader be available?
items.a: The PC Reader is awaiting Microsoft Store approval. Email support@mlogictech.com with subject "Notify me: BiblioFuse PC Reader" and we will let you know when it launches.

productFamily.desc: Built by Modern Logic Tech Solutions for readers and collectors who keep their library on their own devices - not behind someone else's login.

reader.desc: Native reader & library manager for personal e-book and comic collections.
reader.bullet1: EPUB, PDF, CBZ, CBR, RAR, ZIP & TXT
reader.bullet3: iCloud library across iPhone, iPad & Mac

webTool.desc: Convert, merge and compress e-book files in your browser - no install, fully on-device.
webTool.bullet1: EPUB / PDF / CBZ / ZIP merge & convert
webTool.bullet3: Works on any platform with a browser

archive.desc: Finds and removes duplicate comic book archives, photos, and files - even when renamed, recompressed, or saved in different folders.
archive.bullet3: Hash cache for fast repeat scans

smartdecrypt.desc: Unlocks password-protected PDFs and archives using saved filename patterns.

contentcue.desc: Lightweight reading browser that restores your scroll position after any interruption.
contentcue.bullet1: One-tap resume after popups or redirects

readerSection.title: Built for long reading sessions.
readerSection.desc: A focused reader with the library tools you actually use: formats, folders, tags, ratings, progress, and bookmarks. The same library on iPhone, iPad and Mac, kept in sync through your iCloud.

library.title: One shelf for every format.
library.desc: EPUB, PDF, CBZ, CBR, RAR, ZIP and TXT - read in a single library with folders, search and recent books. Import from Files, document picker, or your iCloud Drive.
library.bullet1Body: organize without folders if you want to.
library.bullet2Body: every book remembers where you left off.
library.bullet3Body: pick up on iPhone where you stopped on iPad.
library.imageAlt: BiblioFuse library with tags, ratings and reading progress

reading.title: TTS, auto-scroll & peek zoom.
reading.desc: A distraction-free reader with the controls that matter for long sessions. Text-to-speech for EPUB and TXT. Auto-scroll for comics and webtoons. Peek zoom to inspect a panel without losing your place.
reading.bullet1Body: hands-free narration for EPUB and TXT.
reading.bullet2Body: paged or continuous, tuned for image archives.
reading.bullet3Body: double-tap to inspect dense panels and small text.
reading.imageAlt: BiblioFuse reading view with TTS, auto-scroll and peek zoom

streaming.title: Library hub. Streams to your iPhone.
streaming.desc: On Mac, BiblioFuse becomes a desktop library hub: manage local folders, iCloud folders and SMB network shares in a split-view library/reader interface. Then stream books straight to BiblioFuse on iPhone and iPad over local Wi-Fi at home, or remotely over 5G when you're out.
streaming.bullet1Body: instant browse from any Mac source.
streaming.bullet2Body: open your home library from anywhere with Tailscale.
streaming.bullet3Body: one Mac library, every folder you already have.
streaming.bullet4Body: lock streaming access on shared networks.
streaming.imageAlt: BiblioFuse Mac library with split-view reader
streaming.footnote: Remote access requires iCloud signed in and Tailscale connected on both Mac and iOS. We also recommend using Tailscale for local Wi-Fi connections by selecting the device name with "(iCloud)".

toolsHighlight.title: Compress & merge without leaving the app.
toolsHighlight.onDevice: All processing runs on-device. Nothing uploaded.

resize.title: Resize to shortest side
resize.body: Set a target shortest dimension and re-page every image in your archive.
resize.label: Typical preset

convert.title: Convert to WebP or JPG
convert.body: Re-encode pages to WebP or JPG for smaller archives that still look crisp.
convert.label: Smaller files

merge.title: Merge chapters & series
merge.body: Combine chapters, volumes or split downloads into one clean CBZ, ZIP or PDF.
merge.label: One pass

archiveSection.desc: Archive Duplicate Scanner finds and removes duplicate comic book archives, photos, and files - even when they have been renamed, recompressed, or saved in different folders.

scanning.title: Comic archive scanning
scanning.body: CBZ, CBR, ZIP and RAR using perceptual hashing.

photo.title: Photo duplicate detection
photo.body: JPG, PNG, HEIC, WEBP, BMP and GIF.

select.title: Smart auto-select
select.body: Keep largest, smallest, newest, oldest, or most pages.

delete.title: Safe deletion
delete.body: Marked files move to system Trash only.

archiveSection.altBadge: Download Archive Duplicate Scanner on the Mac App Store
archiveSection.altImage: Archive Duplicate Scanner configure and start scan
archiveSection.pricing: Free usage is available forever for scans up to 1,000 files. A 7-day trial unlocks full access, and a one-time purchase unlocks unlimited use with no subscription.

decryptSection.desc: SmartDecrypt helps you unlock password-protected PDFs and archives without hunting through notes, messages, or old emails for the right password.

formats.title: PDF · ZIP · 7z
formats.body: Unlock protected PDFs and extract ZIP/7z archives.

batch.title: Batch upgrade
batch.body: One-time Pro unlocks batch loading and password cycling.

keychain.title: Keychain-safe
keychain.body: Passwords stay in the Apple Keychain.

decryptSection.altBadge: Download SmartDecrypt PDF ZIP on the App Store
decryptSection.pricing: Free users can unlock one file at a time with pattern matching. SmartDecrypt Pro is a one-time universal purchase that unlocks batch file loading and automatic password cycling on iOS and macOS.
decryptSection.langNote: App UI: English · Spanish · Portuguese · Indonesian · Simplified Chinese

contentcueSection.titleB: Resume exactly where you left off.
contentcueSection.desc: ContentCue is a lightweight reading browser for long articles, documentation, forums, and comics. Popups, redirects, and accidental taps can't steal your place — one tap gets you back.

resume.title: One-tap Resume
resume.body: Returns to your saved scroll position instantly.

adOutlines.title: Visual ad outlines
adOutlines.body: Marks likely ad elements for distraction-free reading.

mute.title: Audio mute
mute.body: Silence auto-playing audio without leaving the page.

bookmarks.title: Bookmarks
bookmarks.body: Save pages to revisit — no account required.

contentcueSection.altBadge: Download ContentCue on the App Store
contentcueSection.pricing: Free to use. ContentCue Pro is a $2.99 one-time purchase: unlimited bookmarks, custom ad outline styles, and adjustable auto-scroll.
contentcueSection.langNote: App UI: English · Spanish · Portuguese · Indonesian · Simplified Chinese
contentcueSection.platformNote: iPhone & iPad · Apple Silicon Mac (iOS app via App Store)

privacyStrip.title: Built on a few simple principles.

noTracking.title: No tracking
noTracking.body: We do not collect or sell your reading data.

cloudSync.title: Cloud sync
cloudSync.body: Sync your library across Apple devices with iCloud.

compression.title: Smart compression
compression.body: Reduce file size and save storage without leaving the app.

finalCta.title: Your library. Your device. Your rules.
finalCta.desc: Start with the Reader. Add the utilities when you need them. Everything stays on-device.

seo.homeTitle: BiblioFuse — Private ebook & comic reader for iOS, macOS, Android & PC
seo.homeDesc: BiblioFuse is a private ebook and comic reader for iOS, macOS, Android, and Windows PC. Read EPUB, PDF, CBZ, CBR offline. Stream your Mac library to iPhone over 5G. No ads, no tracking.
seo.webTitle: Free online EPUB, PDF & CBZ compressor and converter — BiblioFuse Web Tool
seo.webDesc: Compress, merge and convert EPUB, PDF, CBZ and ZIP files entirely in your browser using WebAssembly. No upload, no account. Free for up to 3 files per batch.
seo.aboutTitle: About BiblioFuse — Private ebook and comic reading tools
seo.aboutDesc: BiblioFuse is built by Modern Logic Tech Solutions to give readers full ownership of their library. Private, on-device apps for iOS, macOS, Android, and Windows. No subscription required.
seo.privacyTitle: BiblioFuse Privacy Policy — No ads, no tracking, no account
seo.privacyDesc: BiblioFuse processes all reading, compression, streaming, and duplicate-scanning on your own device or iCloud. We collect no personal data and run no ads.
seo.blogTitle: BiblioFuse Blog — Tips, guides & release notes
seo.blogDesc: How-to guides, release notes, and tips for getting the most out of BiblioFuse Reader, the Web Tool, Archive Duplicate Scanner, and SmartDecrypt.
seo.changelogDesc: Full release history, bug fixes, known issues, and upcoming features for BiblioFuse Reader on iOS, macOS, Android, and Windows PC.
appFeatures: EPUB, PDF, CBZ, CBR, RAR, ZIP and TXT reader
Mac home library streaming to iPhone and iPad over Wi-Fi or remote 5G with Tailscale
SMB access via Mac streaming on iOS and native SMB on Android
On-device compression and merge tools to save phone storage
CBZ, CBR, ZIP and RAR duplicate scanner using sampled image-page perceptual hashing
Private WebAssembly web tool with local browser processing
No ads, no tracking and no account required
